reforms.description: In response to the coronavirus pandemic, states waived many occupational licensing requirements, allowing out-of-State licensed professionals to obtain temporary emergency licenses to practice, and permitting retired health workers or students close to graduation to practice temporarily without a license.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the availability of healthcare professionals during a crisis supports public health and economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reforms are specific to a temporary situation and do not represent a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: States responded to the crisis by waiving many licensing requirements, allowing out-of-State licensed professionals to obtain temporary emergency licenses.

reforms.description: The U.S. government has initiated inquiries into the competitiveness of large technology companies to adapt antitrust policy to new trends in digitalisation and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Adapting antitrust policy to foster competition can enhance innovation and consumer welfare.
reforms.status_evidence: The inquiries have been officially launched and are ongoing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at addressing the challenges posed by large technology firms.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Trade Commission and Department of Justice have initiated broad-ranging inquiries and investigations into the competitiveness of U.S. technology and digital markets.

reforms.description: The federal government, through the Comprehensive Economic Development Strategy run by the Department of Commerce’s Economic Development Administration, provides support for economic development plans, enhancing coordination and limiting the enforceability of non-compete agreements to improve worker mobility and entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting economic development and enhancing worker mobility fosters entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The support mechanisms and reforms to non-compete agreements are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms are significant in promoting entrepreneurship but do not represent a systemic overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government through the Comprehensive Economic Development Strategy run by the Department of Commerce’s Economic Development Administration can provide support for economic development plans.

reforms.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) operates the Connect America Fund to improve broadband provision for all Americans, particularly in rural areas, using reverse auctions to allocate subsidies for expanding coverage.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband access enhances connectivity, which is essential for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund is actively operating and allocating subsidies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a major investment in infrastructure critical for supporting innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The FCC operates the Connect America Fund to improve broadband provision for all Americans including in rural areas at reasonably comparable prices.
